title: Invisible text when scrolling with Ctrl+p/n
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When using <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>p</kbd> and <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>n</kbd> to scroll bash history in a container, text is often invisible. Does not seem to happen when using arrow keys <kbd>↑</kbd> and <kbd>↓</kbd>.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Get ubuntu container with `podman pull ubuntu:18.04`
2. Enter into it with `podman run -it ubuntu:18.04 /bin/bash`
3. In the container, enter three separate commands, so they'd appear in the history: `echo hello1` <kbd>Enter</kbd>, `echo hello2` <kbd>Enter</kbd>, `echo hello3` <kbd>Enter</kbd>.
4. Try scrolling between the three commands back and forth with <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>p</kbd> and <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>n</kbd>

**Describe the results you received:**

The scroll often look like it didn't happen. It actually has happened, it's just invisible. You can make it visible by pressing e.g. <kbd>←</kbd>

**Describe the results you expected:**

The scroll happens as usual, and history entries are visible.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

When scrolling with <kbd>↑</kbd> and <kbd>↓</kbd>, problem does not seem to happen.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Git Commit:   9f6d6ba0b314d86521b66183c9ce48eaa2da1de2
Built:        Sat Sep 26 18:50:37 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
I guess this a duplicate of #5979.

See this for a possible fix: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md#22-containers-default-detach-keys-conflict-with-shell-history-navigation

> I guess this a duplicate of #5979.
> 
> See this for a possible fix: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md#22-containers-default-detach-keys-conflict-with-shell-history-navigation

Oh, right, thank you very much, that wasn't obvious. TL;DR for future readers, the fix was to create a file `~/.config/containers/containers.conf` with the following content:

```
[engine]
detach_keys="ctrl-q,ctrl-q"
```
